title: Horseshoe Kidney: A Systematic Approach
objectives: Understand the pathophysiology of Horseshoe Kidney
Identify key diagnostic features of Horseshoe Kidney
Apply clinical reasoning to Horseshoe Kidney management
Recognize common pitfalls in Horseshoe Kidney

sections.title: Overview of Horseshoe Kidney
sections.content:
**Horseshoe Kidney**

Fusion of lower poles, inferior mesenteric artery entrapment, Turner syndrome association

**Key Points:**
• Lower poles fuse across midline during ascent from pelvis
• Gets trapped under inferior mesenteric artery (IMA) — cannot ascend to normal position
• Usually asymptomatic, found incidentally

sections.title: Pathophysiology & Key Features
sections.content:
• Lower poles fuse across midline during ascent from pelvis
• Gets trapped under inferior mesenteric artery (IMA) — cannot ascend to normal position

sections.title: Diagnosis & Management
sections.content:
• Usually asymptomatic, found incidentally
• Complications: UPJ obstruction, stones, UTIs, increased Wilms tumor risk
• Associated with Turner syndrome (45,X), trisomies 18 and 21

sections.title: Clinical Pearls
sections.content:
**High-Yield Points for Horseshoe Kidney:**

• Usually asymptomatic, found incidentally
• Complications: UPJ obstruction, stones, UTIs, increased Wilms tumor risk
• Associated with Turner syndrome (45,X), trisomies 18 and 21

mcqs.question: Which of the following is MOST characteristic of Horseshoe Kidney?

options.text: Lower poles fuse across midline during ascent from pelvis
options.explanation: Lower poles fuse across midline during ascent from pelvis

options.text: Requires invasive testing in all cases

mcqs.question: What is the KEY diagnostic or management consideration for Horseshoe Kidney?

options.text: Associated with Turner syndrome (45,X), trisomies 18 and 21
options.explanation: Associated with Turner syndrome (45,X), trisomies 18 and 21

options.text: Immediate surgical intervention in all cases

mcqs.question: A patient presents with findings consistent with Horseshoe Kidney. Which finding would you expect?

options.text: Gets trapped under inferior mesenteric artery (IMA) — cannot ascend to normal position
options.explanation: Gets trapped under inferior mesenteric artery (IMA) — cannot ascend to normal position
